components.title: Wireless Access Point (Enterprise WiFi)
components.description: Enterprise-grade wireless access points managed by a central wireless LAN controller (WLC) or cloud management platform. Provides IEEE 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6) coverage for organisation endpoints. Separate SSIDs are configured for corporate devices (802.1X authentication), guest network (isolated, internet-only), and IoT devices (segmented VLAN). WPA3-Enterprise with 802.1X is mandatory for the corporate SSID.

props.name: authentication
props.value: WPA3-Enterprise with 802.1X for corporate SSID; WPA3-Personal for guest

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Cloud management controller or WLC web interface

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — Secure CLI management access to WLC

protocols.name: capwap
protocols.title: CAPWAP — AP-to-WLC control and data channel

protocols.name: radius
protocols.title: RADIUS — 802.1X wireless authentication to NPS/RADIUS server

protocols.name: snmp
protocols.title: SNMP — Network monitoring of AP and WLC status

protocols.name: dhcp
protocols.title: DHCP — IP assignment for wireless clients

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Wireless Access Point (Enterprise WiFi).

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening configuration is maintained for wireless access points and the WLC, based on the vendor's security hardening guide and ASD wireless network guidance. Hardening includes: disabling legacy protocols (802.11b/g where not required), disabling WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup), disabling SSID broadcast for management networks, configuring management VLAN separation, enabling rogue AP detection, and setting minimum RSSI thresholds to limit coverage footprint.

WPA3-Enterprise with 802.1X and EAP-TLS (or PEAP-MSCHAPv2 with server certificate validation) is used to authenticate all organisation devices to the corporate wireless SSID. Authentication is validated against Active Directory via a RADIUS/NPS server. Devices that fail 802.1X authentication cannot join the corporate SSID. The guest SSID is isolated and provides internet-only access with no path to organisational resources.

Wireless client traffic is segmented by SSID into separate VLANs: corporate devices on the corporate VLAN with firewall-controlled access to organisation resources; guest devices on an isolated guest VLAN with internet-only access; and IoT devices on a dedicated IoT VLAN. Inter-VLAN routing between the corporate and guest/IoT VLANs is blocked at the firewall. Wireless management traffic is on a dedicated management VLAN.

Wireless infrastructure events (802.1X authentication success/failure, rogue AP detections, deauthentication floods, WLC alerts) are forwarded via Syslog to the SIEM for security monitoring and retention.
